channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Tulsi Gabbard on Capitol Hill to get Senate support
item.description: Two of President-elect Donald Trump's most controversial cabinet nominees are on Capitol Hill this week.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump claims GOP 'very open' to keeping 'Dreamers' in US, takes shot at 'very difficult' Dems
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he wants to work with Democrats to work out a deal on how to protect some illegal immigrants protected by a 2012 Obama-era order from deportation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gov. Phil Murphy signs law prohibiting book bans, making New Jersey the latest state to do so
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy signed a law to prohibit public libraries and school libraries from banning books and protect librarians who follow the law against liability.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican-led states are rolling out plans that could aid Trump's mass deportation effort
item.description: Governors and lawmakers in Republican-led states are rolling out plans that could aid President-elect Donald Trump's effort to deport millions of people living in the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Massive U.S. airstrikes on Islamic State militants in Syria were meant in part as a message to the group and a move to ensure that it doesn't try to take advantage of the chaos following the overthrow of President Bashar Assad's government. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In promising to shake up Washington, Trump is in a class of his own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the history of American politics, there's no shortage of presidents who promised to shake up Washington once they got to the White House.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has flip-flopped on abortion policy. His appointees may offer clues to what happens next
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — As Donald Trump’s Cabinet begins to take shape, those on both sides of the abortion debate are watching closely for clues about how his picks might affect reproductive rights policy in the president-elect’s second term.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk joins Trump allies in House GOP pressure campaign over social media bill
item.description: People in President-elect Trump's orbit are pushing Speaker Johnson and House Republicans to take up the Kids Online Safety Act.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Grassley rips Wray's 'failed' leadership at FBI with 11 pages of examples in blistering 'no confidence' letter
item.description: Longtime Republican Sen. Chuck Grassley slammed FBI chiief Christopher Wray for "failed" leadership in a lengthy letter where he expressed "no confidence" in the director.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Kinzinger has 'absolutely no worries' Trump will try to put him in jail
item.description: “First off, the executive branch can’t go after the legislative branch because we embarrassed him. That’s not a sin, that’s not against the law,” Kinzinger said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Boston school admissions policy challenge as Alito, Thomas dissent
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a group of white and Asian parents’ challenge to a temporary admissions policy at Boston’s prestigious “exam schools” over dissents from two of the court’s conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump stepping down from RNC amid Senate speculation
item.description: Lara Trump is stepping down as co-chair of the Republican National Committee (RNC) as she considers replacing Florida Sen. Marco Rubio (R), whom her father-in-law, President-elect Donald Trump, picked to be secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he can't guarantee tariffs won't raise US prices and won't rule out revenge prosecutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said he can't guarantee that his promised tariffs on key U.S. foreign trade partners won't raise prices for American consumers and he suggested once more that some political rivals and federal officials who pursued legal cases against him should be imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says US should stay out of fighting in Syria as opposition forces gain
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday that the U.S. military should stay out of the fast-escalating conflict in Syria, where a dramatic rebel offensive reached the capital and threatened the rule of Syria's Russian- and Iranian-allied president.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, WILL WEISSERT and TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: National nonprofit asks Gov. McMaster to 'intervene' in South Carolina
item.description: A controversial Texas-based nonprofit promising to set up citizen patrols in McColl is now asking for state help.
item.dc:creator: Adam Benson, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's new coalition puts female nominees at forefront
item.description: Women are set to play a key role in President-elect Trump’s administration, as he nominates and appoints a number of them to high-level posts. Trump has so far named eight women to Cabinet-level positions — double the four initially named for his first term.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Hegseth faces senators' concerns not only about his behavior but also his views on women in combat
item.description: Minnesotan Pete Hegseth has spent the week on Capitol Hill trying to reassure Republican senators that he is fit to lead President-elect Donald Trump’s Department of Defense in the wake of high-profile allegations about excessive drinking and sexual assault.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News
